title: Food Cooking Machine Operators and Tenders
description: Operate or tend cooking equipment, such as steam cooking vats, deep fry cookers, pressure cookers, kettles, and boilers, to prepare food products.

tasks.text: Clean, wash, and sterilize equipment and cooking area, using water hoses, cleaning or sterilizing solutions, or rinses.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Read work orders, recipes, or formulas to determine cooking times and temperatures, and ingredient specifications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Observe gauges, dials, and product characteristics, and adjust controls to maintain appropriate temperature, pressure, and flow of ingredients.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Measure or weigh ingredients, using scales or measuring containers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Tend or operate and control equipment, such as kettles, cookers, vats and tanks, and boilers, to cook ingredients or prepare products for further processing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Record production and test data, such as processing steps, temperature and steam readings, cooking time, batches processed, and test results.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Set temperature, pressure, and time controls, and start conveyers, machines, or pumps.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove cooked material or products from equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Collect and examine product samples during production to test them for quality, color, content, consistency, viscosity, acidity, or specific gravity.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Pour, dump, or load prescribed quantities of ingredients or products into cooking equipment, manually or using a hoist.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Listen for malfunction alarms, and shut down equipment and notify supervisors when necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Notify or signal other workers to operate equipment or when processing is complete.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Turn valves or start pumps to add ingredients or drain products from equipment and to transfer products for storage, cooling, or further processing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Admit required amounts of water, steam, cooking oils, or compressed air into equipment, such as by opening water valves to cool mixtures to the desired consistency.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Activate agitators and paddles to mix or stir ingredients, stopping machines when ingredients are thoroughly mixed.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate auxiliary machines and equipment, such as grinders, canners, and molding presses, to prepare or further process products.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Place products on conveyors or carts, and monitor product flow.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.
